summary: Test suite provides decent coverage of script functionality (parse, version bump, changelog) but critically fails to address the primary GitHub Actions requirement. Task explicitly mandates 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT' with a full test harness that executes the workflow for each test case, captures output to act-result.txt, and validates against expected results. Provided tests only run the script directly via bats, never via the GitHub Actions workflow. Missing: (1) GitHub Actions workflow validation/structure tests, (2) actionlint validation tests, (3) act execution and output capture, (4) act-result.txt artifact, (5) Red-green TDD methodology. Design issues: loose assertions in changelog test (`[[ $result == *"feat"* ]]`), no actual git tag testing despite tag-based logic, no test fixtures as separate files. Rigor gaps: no malformed version handling, whitespace edge cases, git state errors, boundary conditions on version numbers. Would not catch regressions in the workflow or validate it runs correctly with act.
